mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, systematically lowering present valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant directional bias (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent confirms labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending expectations and forcing equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real spending growth, where output expands while consumer spending contracts, signals inventory accumulation and weak demand, driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample multivariate regression over a 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent indicates immediate supply-side contraction, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant directional bias (t-stat < 1.5) during tightening regimes, or signal accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, prompting investors to allocate capital to nominal equities and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in more than 70 percent of instances where real rates are negative, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, directly supporting corporate earnings and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth versus 60-day returns falls below 0.01, or forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900 thousand units signals a sharp deceleration in construction demand and household formation, reducing near-term economic momentum and pressuring equity valuations.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of months where HOUST falls below 900.0, or directional accuracy fails to exceed 53 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment confirms a broad-based economic contraction, triggering defensive portfolio shifts and negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict direction with accuracy greater than 55 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
